title: Space Mice: Born from Cosmic Sperm
story: Squeaky space babies! In 2017, Japanese scientists made a groundbreaking discovery. They successfully created mice using sperm that had boldly gone where no sperm had gone before - to the International Space Station! On May 22, researchers from the University of Yamanashi announced that freeze-dried mouse sperm, after orbiting Earth, could still produce healthy pups. This cosmic experiment opened new possibilities for preserving life in space.
activity_question: What did the Japanese scientists use to create the mice?
follow_up_question: Why do you think scientists might want to study how space affects reproduction?

title: Pac-Man Chomps Its Way into Gaming History
story: Wakka-wakka! The hungry yellow circle with a big mouth burst onto the scene in 1980. On May 22, Pac-Man gobbled up the attention of gamers in Japan. Created by Toru Iwatani, this maze-munching hero quickly became an arcade sensation. Players guided Pac-Man through mazes, chomping dots and avoiding colorful ghosts. The simple yet addictive gameplay made Pac-Man a cultural icon that's still recognized today.

bonus_fact: Pac-Man's shape was inspired by a pizza with a slice missing!

title: Lunar Dress Rehearsal: Apollo 10's Close Call
story: Astronauts zoomed closer to the Moon than ever before! On May 22, 1969, Thomas Stafford and Eugene Cernan piloted the Apollo 10 lunar module to a nail-biting 9.4 miles above the Moon's cratered surface. This daring 'dress rehearsal' paved the way for the historic Moon landing just months later. title: A Steamy Voyage Across the Atlantic
story: Puffing clouds of smoke, the SS Savannah chugged out of Georgia's Savannah Harbor on May 22, 1819. This wasn't just any ship - it was about to make waves in history! The Savannah became the first steam-powered vessel to cross the Atlantic Ocean. Though it also used sails, its steam engine marked a new era in sea travel. The journey took nearly a month, showing the world the future of ocean voyages.
activity_question: What made the SS Savannah's journey historic?
activity_choices: It was the first steam-powered vessel to cross the Atlantic
It was the fastest ship of its time
It carried the most passengers ever
activity_answer: It was the first steam-powered vessel to cross the Atlantic
bonus_fact: The SS Savannah used both steam power and sails during its groundbreaking journey.

title: Copernicus' Final Resting Place Found
story: After centuries of mystery, the remains of Nicolaus Copernicus were finally laid to rest. The famous Polish astronomer, who discovered that Earth orbits the Sun, was reburied in Frombork Cathedral, Poland in 2010. Scientists had searched for his tomb for 200 years before solving this cosmic puzzle on May 22.
activity_question: Where were Copernicus' remains reburied?
bonus_fact: Copernicus was the first to propose that planets orbit the Sun, not Earth.

title: Halley's Comet Zooms Past Earth
story: Streaking across the night sky, Halley's Comet made its 14th recorded appearance in 760. This cosmic visitor, a ball of ice and dust, swings by Earth every 75-76 years. Ancient stargazers spotted it way back in 240 BC, but it wasn't until 1705 that Edmund Halley figured out it was the same comet returning again and again. On May 22, 760, it reached its closest point to the Sun, putting on a dazzling show for sky-watchers.

follow_up_question: When is Halley's Comet expected to return next?

title: The World's First Atlas Unfolds

On May 22, Ortelius unveiled his masterpiece, changing how people saw the world forever.
